raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The request to make the passage stronger centrally includes sharpening the contribution framing and what the adaptive MPC framework adds.","R1.2":"Strengthening the passage requires checking that the method, trigger mechanism, evaluation, and stability caveat form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The reported claims about tracking error, robustness, overhead, and deployment need to remain aligned with the stated experiments and limitations.","R1.6":"The most direct responsibility is sentence- and paragraph-level improvement because the artifact has tense inconsistency, long run-on sentences, and register issues.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty around robustness, tuning choices, and the unproven stability assumption.","RX.2":"Avoiding overclaim is load-bearing because the artifact makes performance and deployment claims while acknowledging an assumed stability result.","RX.3":"The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation, not executing edits or evaluating beyond the requested strengthening scope.","RX.4":"Because the artifact concerns real-time control of nonlinear plants, the projection should account for downstream risk from overstated safety or stability claims.","RX.5":"Traceability matters so that any projected responsibility remains grounded in the claims and caveats present in the supplied artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The request to make the passage stronger centrally includes sharpening the contribution framing and what the adaptive MPC framework adds.
rationale.R1.2: Strengthening the passage requires checking that the method, trigger mechanism, evaluation, and stability caveat form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The reported claims about tracking error, robustness, overhead, and deployment need to remain aligned with the stated experiments and limitations.
rationale.R1.6: The most direct responsibility is sentence- and paragraph-level improvement because the artifact has tense inconsistency, long run-on sentences, and register issues.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty around robustness, tuning choices, and the unproven stability assumption.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaim is load-bearing because the artifact makes performance and deployment claims while acknowledging an assumed stability result.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation, not executing edits or evaluating beyond the requested strengthening scope.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns real-time control of nonlinear plants, the projection should account for downstream risk from overstated safety or stability claims.
rationale.RX.5: Traceability matters so that any projected responsibility remains grounded in the claims and caveats present in the supplied artifact.
